question_text: Calculate the pressure exerted by a force of 60 newtons acting over an area of 3 square metres. Use the formula pressure = force / area.
explanation: Using the formula pressure = force / area, we divide 60 N by 3 m² to get 20 Pa. This shows how pressure depends on both the force applied and the area it acts over; the same force spread over a larger area creates less pressure.
